Name: dynamic_kv_cache_layout
Title: Dynamic KV-Cache Layout Optimization for Efficient Transformer Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic adjustment of the KV-cache layout based on input sequence characteristics can significantly improve memory bandwidth utilization and reduce latency in transformer inference, without requiring retraining or architectural changes.
Related Work: Existing KV-cache optimization methods focus on static layouts or fixed compression strategies, which fail to adapt to varying input sequence lengths and patterns. Recent work like Spectral Quantization Routing explores dynamic precision allocation but does not address KV-cache layout. Our proposal uniquely targets dynamic layout adjustments based on real-time sequence characteristics, bridging the gap between static optimizations and input-aware adaptations.
Abstract: Transformer inference efficiency is heavily influenced by the KV-cache layout, which impacts memory bandwidth utilization and latency. Current approaches use static layouts, leading to suboptimal performance across varying input sequences. We propose a dynamic KV-cache layout optimization framework that adjusts the layout based on real-time sequence characteristics, such as token sparsity and sequence length. Our method employs a lightweight heuristic to determine the optimal layout for each input sequence, ensuring efficient memory access patterns and reduced latency. We implement this framework using CUDA/Triton kernels, enabling seamless integration with existing transformer architectures. Empirical evaluations on commodity GPUs demonstrate significant latency reductions and improved memory bandwidth utilization across diverse benchmarks, with minimal overhead.
Experiments: Benchmark latency and memory bandwidth utilization on standard transformer models (e.g., GPT-2, BERT) with static KV-cache layouts.
Implement dynamic KV-cache layout adjustment based on token sparsity and sequence length, using CUDA/Triton kernels.
Compare latency, memory bandwidth utilization, and throughput between static and dynamic layouts on a range of input sequences.
Profile kernel performance using nvprof/Nsight to analyze occupancy, warp stall reasons, and arithmetic intensity.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of different sequence characteristics on layout optimization effectiveness.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Dynamic layout adjustments may introduce overhead that outweighs the benefits for certain input sequences.
The heuristic for determining the optimal layout may not generalize well across diverse models and tasks.
Integration with existing transformer architectures may require significant engineering effort.
The proposed method may not achieve significant improvements on models with already highly optimized KV-cache layouts.
